race.comment: With a few key pace influences coming out, we pivot to TORI'S DEE (8) at a big price. Jamie Edwards has 7yo gelding in outstanding form, winning his past four up on speed, all on very wet tracks. This is tougher again but he looks the likely leader and that is worth plenty around here. JUGIONG (11) looked home at Caulfield Heath last time before just being nosed out late. If he can hold a spot early he'll take beating. CINTURATO (13) comes back in trip off a let-up but has the peaks to win and will take holding out, just worry if he's quite sharp enough at 1200m to pick them up but he's talented enough. HUMZA BEY (19) resumes. He's a lightly raced gelding who profiles well albeit draws wide and may need a touch further to show his best.
